strengths: Demonstrated proper needle driving technique through the Penrose drain
Successfully completed extracorporeal knot formation with appropriate throws
Maintained good instrument control throughout the suturing sequence
weaknesses: Knot slipped under tension indicating insufficient knot security
May have inadequate tension management during knot tightening
Possible issues with throw sequence or knot pusher technique

drills.name: Knot-pusher sequencing drill
drills.description: Practice 10 three-throw extracorporeal sequences with deliberate pusher advancement and controlled tension application.

drills.name: Tension control practice
drills.description: Tie knots with varying tension levels, testing each knot's security before advancing to ensure proper holding strength.

drills.name: Double-then-single sequence drill
drills.description: Rehearse one surgeon's knot followed by two single throws without time pressure, focusing on knot security over speed.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, knot security must be prioritized over speed. Focus on consistent throw mechanics and proper tension application to prevent slippage before pursuing faster completion times.
